2603.14420.title: Data Darwinism Part II: DataEvolve -- AI can Autonomously Evolve Pretraining Data Curation
2603.14420.paper_summary: Data Darwinism (Part I) established a ten-level hierarchy for data processing, showing that stronger processing can unlock greater data value. However, that work relied on manually designed strategies for a single category. Modern pretraining corpora comprise hundreds of heterogeneous categories spanning domains and content types, each demanding specialized treatment. At this scale, manual strategy design becomes prohibitive. This raises a key question: can strategies evolve in an automated way? We introduce DataEvolve, a framework that enables strategies to evolve through iterative optimization rather than manual design. For each data category, DataEvolve operates in a closed evolutionary loop: it identifies quality issues, generates candidate strategies, executes them on sampled data, evaluates results, and refines approaches across generations. The process accumulates knowledge through an experience pool of discovered issues and a strategy pool tracking performance across iterations. Applied to 8 categories spanning 672B tokens from Nemotron-CC, DataEvolve produces Darwin-CC, a 504B-token dataset with strategies evolved through 30 iterations per category. Training 3B models on 500B tokens, Darwin-CC outperforms raw data (+3.96 points) and achieves a 44.13 average score across 18 benchmarks, surpassing DCLM, Ultra-FineWeb, and FineWeb-Edu, with strong gains on knowledge-intensive tasks such as MMLU. Analysis shows evolved strategies converge on cleaning-focused approaches: targeted noise removal and format normalization with domain-aware preservation, echoing the L4 (Generative Refinement) principles from Part I. Ablation studies confirm iterative evolution is essential: optimized strategies outperform suboptimal ones by 2.93 points, establishing evolutionary strategy design as feasible and necessary for pretraining-scale data curation.
2603.14420.paper_summary_zh: 數據達爾文主義（第一部分）建立了一個十層級的數據處理層次結構，顯示出更強的處理能力可以解鎖更大的數據價值。<lb/>然而，那項工作依賴於為單一類別手動設計的策略。<lb/>現代的預訓練語料庫包含數百個異質類別，涵蓋不同的領域和內容類型，每個類別都需要專門的處理。<lb/>在這個規模下，手動策略設計變得不可行。<lb/>這引發了一個關鍵問題：策略能否以自動化的方式進化？<lb/>我們介紹了DataEvolve，一個通過迭代優化而非手動設計使策略進化的框架。<lb/>對於每個數據類別，DataEvolve在一個封閉的進化循環中運作：它識別質量問題，生成候選策略，在抽樣數據上執行這些策略，評估結果，並在世代之間精煉方法。<lb/>這個過程通過發現問題的經驗池和跟踪迭代性能的策略池積累知識。<lb/>應用於涵蓋672B標記的8個類別，DataEvolve生成了Darwin-CC，這是一個擁有504B標記的數據集，策略經過每個類別30次迭代進化而來。<lb/>在500B標記上訓練3B模型，Darwin-CC的表現超過了原始數據（+3.96分），並在18個基準測試中達到44.13的平均分，超越了DCLM、Ultra-FineWeb和FineWeb-Edu，在如MMLU等知識密集型任務上有強勁的增長。<lb/>分析顯示，進化的策略集中於清理為重點的方法：針對性噪聲去除和格式標準化，並在保留領域知識的情況下進行，這呼應了第一部分的L4（生成性精煉）原則。<lb/>消融研究確認了迭代進化是必不可少的：優化策略的表現比次優策略高出2.93分，確立了進化策略設計對於預訓練規模數據策展的可行性和必要性。
2603.14420.author: Tiantian Mi et.al.
2603.14420.authors: Tiantian Mi, Dongming Shan, Zhen Huang, Yiwei Qin, Muhang Xie, Yuxuan Qiao, Yixiu Liu, Chenyang Zhou, Pengfei Liu
